category_title: Children who hardly speak or do not speak at all
minimal_context: The student and therapist are looking at toys during a picture-naming exercise to describe what is happening.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Look at this picture of the family with the toys. Tell me what is happening here.

STUDENT: Papa.

TUTOR: Okay, what else?

STUDENT: Ja. (Uses a deictic gesture to point at another toy in the picture)

TUTOR: Can you tell me more about that?

STUDENT: Nee. (Makes an iconic hand movement without speech, relying on a one-word sentence because they cannot verbally express the complex concept they have in mind)

options.A: Conduct functional behavior analyses targeting purported variables and subject them to data-based hypothesis testing during therapy sessions.
options.B: Socialize the student into expectations around turn-taking by providing an 'interaction envelope' around their conduct during picture-naming exercises.
options.C: Support meaningful words with gestures and signs while carrying out therapy activities like picture-naming exercises and describing toys.
options.D: Create only rules absolutely necessary for a safe therapy environment; if sitting perfectly still is unimportant, do not require it.
